title: Pani Puri Masala

ingredients.title: Dry Spice Blend
items: Dry Kashmiri red chilies – 2–3 (for color, mild heat)

instruction.title: Make the Dry Masala
steps: Dry roast cumin, coriander, fennel, black pepper, chilies, cloves, and cinnamon in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium heat until fragrant.
Cool and grind to a fine powder.
Mix in black salt, regular salt, dry ginger powder, amchur, and hing.
Store in an airtight jar — keeps for 2–3 months.

instruction.title: Prepare the Pani (Spiced Water)
steps: In a blender, grind mint, coriander, green chilies, ginger, and tamarind pulp to a smooth paste using a little water.
Strain the paste if you want a clearer, restaurant-style pani.

instruction.title: Mix the Pani
steps: In a large jug, combine fresh paste, 2–3 tbsp prepared pani puri masala, lemon juice, and 1.5 liters chilled water.
Mix well, taste, and adjust salt, tang (lemon/amchur), or heat (chili).
Refrigerate at least 1 hour before serving for flavors to mature.

chefTips: Ice Cold Serving: Always serve pani with ice cubes or from the fridge — it sharpens flavor.
Balance: Keep the dry masala slightly stronger than needed; dilution with water adjusts it perfectly.
Secret Touch: Some chefs add a pinch of jaggery (gur) to balance the sharp tang and spice.
Variations: Offer two types of pani at restaurants:
Meetha pani (sweet-sour tamarind-jaggery water with light masala).
This masala isn’t just for pani puri — you can also use it in jaljeera, chaas, fruit chaat, or spicy lemonade.
